trivia_bullets.text: Angel of Renewal is a White Uncommon from Battle for Zendikar (BFZ), released October 2, 2015. Its creature type is Angel Ally — a deliberate hybrid: Angels are Zendikar's native divine defenders, while Ally is the BFZ set mechanic creature type representing the cross-species coalition of mortals banding together against the Eldrazi invasion. The card thus sits at the thematic intersection of the set's two defining factions. [Scryfall]

Flavor text: "No more fear. No more failure. No more death. No more!" [Scryfall] — an unnamed speaker but tonally consistent with a rallying cry at the height of Zendikar's last stand against the Eldrazi. The repetition and terminal 'No more!' read as a combat declaration. The card has never been reprinted; this BFZ print is the only version. [Scryfall: single print confirmed via prints_search_uri]

The card costs 5W (6 mana total) for a 4/4 Flying body that gains you 1 life for each creature you control when it enters — including itself. In a wide Ally board state or token strategy, this can generate substantial life totals. EDHREC rank 15,886 as of the Scryfall fetch indicates modest Commander adoption; the card is legal in Pioneer, Modern, Legacy, Vintage, and Commander. [Scryfall: edhrec_rank, legalities]

Illustrated by Todd Lockwood, whose MTG portfolio is extensive and spans multiple sets. Angel of Renewal has no reprints — it is a BFZ-only card. As a 6-mana uncommon it sits at the high end of BFZ's white curve; the Ally type synergizes with the set's Rally keyword (abilities that trigger whenever an Ally enters the battlefield), though Angel of Renewal itself does not have a Rally ability, making it more of a cap creature than a Rally engine. [Scryfall; Draftsim: BFZ set overview, via WebSearch]
sources: Draftsim: BFZ set overview, via WebSearch
